objective: Establish a governed multi-user research access boundary before MCP, reports, cards, the Web App, or chat expose personal research state.
claim_ceiling: Prepared authorization-contract corpus only; no code, schema, authentication route, identity-provider binding, browser or MCP access, report or card mutation, replay submission, factor execution, Qlib invocation, source call, stable handoff, or release activation is accepted.

requirements: the #229 capability registration and A0 batch registration are merged to main before this prepared revision is accepted
#81 has accepted the actual-diff, merge-base, corpus-byte, integration-lease, command-evidence, and handoff enforcement required to activate queued batches
this preparation freezes the access corpus path and SHA-256 without changing implementation paths
the stable activation base remains 2fe3bc7e43c89b317caceeb4d627657cf7c50729 while implementation evidence records its exact current PR base and head
the independent reviewer remains assigned through E0 acceptance
one active integration lease covers init.md, the contract export, the additive migration, db/roles.sql, and the SQL contract test
issue #230 contains this manifest path and exact SHA-256
Delivery Governance CI passes offline validation and live GitHub parity

later_claim_dependencies: #41 must publish an accepted E2 stable typed mart-read handoff before #42, #43, or #45 implement against this access handoff
#225, #226, and #227 must each publish their resolved implementation design before their conversation, document, or Web work is scheduled
#66 owns deployed authentication, entitlement, privacy, and consumer validation; this batch makes no Production claim
a named product/legal owner must approve retention, deletion, export, and break-glass policy before any user-content retention or exception is activated

corpus.environment: Local/CI checked-in synthetic authorization fixtures only
required_strata: two isolated tenants with positive owner reads and owner-only conversation and document records
one permitted and one denied immutable publication class for materialized research
administrator access to permitted strategy/backtest results and non-content audit metadata only
administrator denial for private conversation/document content
missing, invalid, expired, revoked, and forged role/tier authentication contexts
valid browser-session, delegated-MCP, administrator, and service-identity authentication contexts
cross-tenant guessed conversation and document object IDs with denial before any mart SQL, private-row lookup, or artifact retrieval
append-only grant, revocation, publication-policy, allowed-access, denied-access, and non-content audit event histories

source_ceiling: Checked-in typed and SQL fixtures only through E2; no network identity-provider call, credential, live source, data refresh, or external MCP client is used.

release_activation.reason: This batch creates only an authorization contract and private-state storage boundary. No ReleaseManifest, default route, schedule, consumer binding, or production identity provider may select it.

negative_controls: missing, expired, revoked, cross-tenant, or client-supplied access identity reaches a mart query, artifact retrieval, or mutation
administrator reads private conversation or document content without separately approved break-glass evidence
grant, revocation, publication policy, or access audit row is updated or deleted instead of appended
private app rows bypass RLS or return after cross-tenant object-ID guessing
raw or staging access, arbitrary SQL, model-generated SQL, factor computation, Qlib invocation, or replay execution is introduced
AccessContext, tenant, principal, role, entitlement, or publication-policy metadata enters factor, BacktestDataGateway, DecisionSnapshot, ReplayEventStream, or Qlib types
a default route, release binding, scheduler, live credential, identity-provider request, or Production readiness claim is activated

output.retention: E0/E1 evidence follows CI retention; the accepted E2 contract, handoff, policy identities, and audit schema are retained permanently.

invalidation: AccessContext identity grain, authentication method, action/resource vocabulary, publication-policy identity, RLS context protocol, append-only event semantics, retention policy, or corpus change creates a new batch revision and reruns affected rungs
a change to App-direct-mart, LLM/MCP typed-read, factor/backtest isolation, or source-budget constraints requires an init.md amendment before implementation
a release, route, identity-provider, credential, sharing, export, break-glass, or replay-execution requirement expands scope and requires a separate authorized batch
a revoked grant or policy preserves historical audit/evidence but blocks new authorization decisions under that grant or policy
rollback: Revert the active batch revision and revoke any produced handoff before consumer activation. The additive app schema remains backward compatible; authorization, grant, revocation, publication-policy, and audit records are never mutated or deleted.
